reasoning: Le Quang Liem’s current FIDE Elo from the April 1, 2026 list is 2731. A 1% increase requires an Elo of 2759, which translates to moving into the top 6 in the world. Liem, currently 35 (born 1991) and semi-active as an elite classical player mostly due to his coaching duties at Webster University, has never achieved this rating. For the short-term horizons (2026), reaching 2759 is virtually impossible, particularly for April 19, which will rely on the static April list. Jumping 28 points would necessitate winning multiple super-tournaments overwhelmingly. As the horizon stretches to 1, 3, 5, and 10 years, factors like potential Elo rating inflation increase the upside risk, though counterbalanced by the fact that he will age into his 40s (players typically see classical rating declines past 40). Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon and shift towards 0.5, probabilities are steeply graded from 5% up to 40% for 2036.
